disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Sudden inability to mobilise safely at home in the two days since hospital discharge, with the patient now staying in bed
trigger_question_examples: What has changed since you came home from hospital?
How are you managing to move around the house?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Since I got home two days ago I just can't get myself up. I can't even make it to the toilet on my own now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Intense fear of falling driving the immobility, and distress about coping and independence, in an alert and oriented person
trigger_question_examples: What is worrying you most right now?
What happens when you try to stand up?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm terrified I'll fall and not be able to get up, and end up in a nursing home. I'm too scared to even try to walk.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new breathlessness at rest beyond baseline, no fever, no increased or discoloured sputum, still speaking in full sentences
trigger_question_examples: Is your breathing any worse than your normal?
Any fever, or more phlegm than usual, or a change in its colour?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, my breathing is about the same as it always is. No fever, and my phlegm is the same as usual.
disclosure_items.patient_deflection_template: I mean my chest is always a bit rough, but it's no different to normal.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Painful, stiff knees that limit standing and walking
trigger_question_examples: Are your knees or legs bothering you?
Does anything hurt when you try to stand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My knees are stiff and sore and they don't want to hold me up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home environment is cluttered with floor hazards and the bathroom has no grab rails; the patient is at high risk of a fall
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me about the home — are there things on the floor, or rails in the bathroom?
Is the path to the toilet clear?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The house is a bit cluttered and there are no rails in the bathroom. I have to shuffle past things to get anywhere.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: The live-in adult child is providing near-constant help and is becoming exhausted
trigger_question_examples: Who is helping you at home?
How is your family member coping with the extra help you need?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My son is helping me with everything but I can see he's worn out, and I feel like a burden.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No new chest pain and no calf pain or one-sided leg swelling
trigger_question_examples: Any pain, swelling or redness in one calf more than the other?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain at all, and my legs aren't swollen or sore in one spot — they just feel weak all over.

patient_initiated_exchanges.trigger_condition: At any point where the patient fears hospital or institutionalisation
patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Please don't send me back to hospital or put me in a home — I just want to stay here. Can you sort out some help to come to me?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Acknowledge the fear, reassure that she is not medically unwell tonight, and explain that the right pathway is an urgent in-person GP assessment to set up a coordinated care plan and in-home allied health support — not an emergency department. The AI should not itself promise to draft the care plan or referrals.
